triaged.whatItRenders: The supercomputer "neurology" INSIDE the brain silhouette: a wired graph of glowing compute nodes (3 draw calls). (1) InstancedMesh of IcosahedronGeometry energy-spheres — 5 hubs (4 real anatomical work-anchors CAUSAL/ARCHIVE/LATTICE/SIGNAL + 1 authored central ROUTER) plus tier-budgeted satellites (24/16/3 per hub) or real knowledge-graph/skill-trail nodes; each node is a region-hued core + fresnel halo that fires per-hub on live cognition-bus events (aActivation) and ignites router-outward during arrival coalescence (aIgniteDelay). (2) Merged TubeGeometry intra-cluster edges with fiber-optic packets racing along them. (3) Merged TubeGeometry backbone bus (ROUTER->each hub). All additive, depthWrite/depthTest off so it x-rays through the glass cranium.
triaged.mountEvidence: SuperbrainScene.tsx:963-965 `{NODE_BRAIN && <NodeLattice uniforms={uniforms} tier={tier} reducedMotion={reduceMotion} />}`, gated by `const NODE_BRAIN = BEING_MODE === 'points'` at SuperbrainScene.tsx:725. Mounts in the official points path with NO GPU gating — so it ALSO mounts under ?gpu=webgpu (alongside GpuBrainPointField at 905-918), where its GLSL materials fail to render. This IS the operator's "in-brain node lattice is invisible under WebGPU" bug.
triaged.materialKind: 3x custom GLSL THREE.ShaderMaterial — nodeMat (NodeLattice.tsx:706, NODE_VERTEX/NODE_FRAGMENT, instanced: reads instanceColor/instanceMatrix + custom instanced attrs aActivation/aIgniteDelay), edgeMat (NodeLattice.tsx:779, BACKBONE_VERTEX/WIRE_FRAGMENT, custom attrs aWireColor/aPhase/aSpeed), backboneMat (NodeLattice.tsx:831, same wire shaders). All AdditiveBlending, transparent, depthWrite:false, depthTest:false, toneMapped:false (node). Geometry: IcosahedronGeometry(1,1) instanced + merged TubeGeometry (LineCurve3/CatmullRomCurve3).
triaged.portStrategy: Port all 3 ShaderMaterials to TSL node materials (import nodes from 'three/tsl', materials from 'three/webgpu' — same split as gpuPointFieldTSL.ts:13-17). NODES (instanced glow spheres): use SpriteNodeMaterial OR MeshBasicNodeMaterial on the same InstancedMesh of IcosahedronGeometry; map per-instance data via storage(...).element(instanceIndex) or attribute() (instanceColor->per-instance vec3 color node; aActivation/aIgniteDelay->storage float buffers updated each frame like gpuPointFieldTSL's StorageInstancedBufferAttribute at :53). Build the fresnel core+rim in a Fn: fres = pow(1 - dot(normalGeometry, normalView clamp), 2.5); colorNode = (core + rim)*uNodeGain + activation surge + arrival ignite smoothstep(igniteDelay, igniteDelay+0.25, 1-uArrival); emit >1 for bloom, set .toneMapped=false. EDGES + BACKBONE (the WIRE_FRAGMENT packet language): MeshBasicNodeMaterial (or LineBasicNodeMaterial if switched to lines); reuse uv() as the travel coord, port the packet math to a Fn — carrier = color*0.08*uCarrierGain; motion=(1-uHold)*(1-uReducedMotion); flow=fract(uv.x*4 - uTime*speed*motion + phase); packet=smoothstep(0.86,0.90,flow)*smoothstep(0.98,0.94,flow); signal=mix(cyan,white,uBurst); arrival wipe via step(uv.x, front); discard at endpoints via the equivalent (alphaTest/opacity 0 mask since WGSL has no discard — use colorNode*mask + transparent). Per-edge aWireColor/aPhase/aSpeed become attribute() vec3/float on the merged TubeGeometry. Drive uniforms with uniform() from 'three/tsl' fed by the existing CognitionUniforms refs (uTime/uBurst/uHold/uArrival/uReducedMotion/uSignalGain/uCarrierGain/uNodeGain). The keepalive useFrame activation-decay loop, real-data fetch, buildLatticeData topology, and subscribeCognition wiring are all renderer-agnostic and stay as-is — only the 3 material constructors change. Reuse refs: GpuBrainPointField.tsx + lib/gpuPointFieldTSL.ts (storage buffers, instanceIndex, uniform(), Fn, SpriteNodeMaterial, sacred-palette color emit) and GpuPostFX.tsx (the >1 emit feeds the same TSL bloom).
triaged.risks: Three distinct shaders to port, two of them sharing the non-trivial fiber-optic WIRE_FRAGMENT packet/wipe math (translate once, reuse). (1) WGSL has no `discard` — the endpoint cut (vUv.x<0.005||>0.995) and the carrier/packet visibility must become a multiplicative mask + transparent/opacity, not discard; getting fully-black masked fragments to not stack additively needs care. (2) Instancing under node materials differs from GLSL #ifdef USE_INSTANCING_COLOR / instanceMatrix injection — per-instance color and the dynamic aActivation buffer must move to storage()/instancedBufferAttribute node access (instanceIndex), and instance normals (mat3(instanceMatrix)) need the node-material instancing path. (3) Additive blending + depthTest:false render-order interplay with the TSL bloom pass must be re-verified so nodes still POP past the bloom knee (NODE_GAIN/SIGNAL_GAIN luma>1). (4) Raw getTexture() black-sampling gotcha doesn't apply (no textures) — good. (5) Keep the WIRE_FRAGMENT in sync note: this is a copy of NervousSystem's; if NervousSystem is also ported, share the TSL Fn. (6) Sacred palette: hues are baked region colors — port must lift only luminance, never shift hue. Must dual-path or fully replace so the default WebGL build still renders (don't regress the working WebGL lattice).

triaged.whatItRenders: The materialized work-slab tabs (the OPERATOR TOP PRIORITY he saw broken). Per surface: an extruded rounded-rectangle dark-glass slab body, a neon-cyan edge frame, a glowing umbilical nerve from the spine vertebra to the tab, traveling light beads along the nerve, a point-field skin (dotted border + interior grain) so the slab reads as one organism with the brain, and luminous 3D code/approval/header text. Three kinds: content (code preview, line-by-line reveal), approval (diff + APPROVE/REJECT buttons), input (brainstem intake — filtered out at the mount site, see below).
triaged.mountEvidence: MaterializationLayer is mounted UNCONDITIONALLY at SuperbrainScene.tsx:967 (`<MaterializationLayer reducedMotion={reduceMotion} />`, outside any POINTS gate). Inside MaterializationLayer.tsx:413-427 the work tabs map renders `<MaterializedTab>` for every surface with `tab.kind !== 'input'` — NOT gated by POINTS. POINTS gating is INTERNAL to MaterializedTab (e.g. const POINTS at line 8; body darkening 1464-1469; cyan frame 1482-1485; OrganPointFieldSkin mounted for points at 1882-1892; conductor fan dropped 1693). So the slab body, frame, umbilical, beads, point-skin and troika text ALL render in the official points path. (Only the per-root conductor tube fan + node/bead/puncta dots at 1693-1814 are {!POINTS} and do NOT mount.)
triaged.materialKind: ALL three.js BUILT-INS, NO custom GLSL. (1) Slab body = meshStandardMaterial on ExtrudeGeometry (line 1834). (2) Frame = lineBasicMaterial on EdgesGeometry (1850), forced to cyan #7bf5fb in points (1483). (3) Points-mode skin (OrganPointFieldSkin, 668-727) = array of sphereGeometry + meshBasicMaterial (additive) meshes — it does NOT use the GLSL createPointFieldMaterial factory (that lives in lib/pointFieldMaterial.ts and is only used by BrainPointField.tsx; grep of MaterializedTab for createPointFieldMaterial/ShaderMaterial/onBeforeCompile = ZERO matches). (4) Umbilical nerve (tubeRef, 1678) = meshStandardMaterial on a TubeGeometry — it is a TUBE MESH, not a Line/LineBasicMaterial. (5) Beads (1816-1827) = meshBasicMaterial spheres. (6) Code/approval text = drei <Text> = troika-three-text (its own MeshBasicMaterial-derived SDF material). Approval buttons (270-340) = meshStandardMaterial/meshBasicMaterial.
triaged.portStrategy: NO GLSL→TSL rewrite needed — this component contains zero custom ShaderMaterial/RawShaderMaterial/onBeforeCompile. Every material is a three.js built-in (meshStandardMaterial, meshBasicMaterial, lineBasicMaterial) which WebGPURenderer renders via its WebGL-compat node path, so the slab, frame, umbilical tube, beads and point-skin should render correctly (this likely contradicts the 'washed-out flat planes' symptom — that is more plausibly the global lighting/tone-mapping/bloom path, GpuPostFX/AgX, not these materials). TWO follow-ups to VERIFY at runtime under ?gpu=webgpu rather than port: (A) meshStandardMaterial is PBR and needs scene LIGHTS + correct tone mapping under WebGPU; if the WebGPU scene has no/different lights or no AgX exposure, the standard-material slab/umbilical/buttons would read flat and 'washed-out' — confirm the lighting + renderOutput/agxToneMapping chain (per the GpuPostFX reference) actually applies to this layer. (B) drei <Text> (troika) patches a base material's shader — confirm troika's shader-chunk patching survives WebGPURenderer (known troika+WebGPU fragility); if glyphs go black/blank, the fallback is to swap troika for a TSL text approach or render text to a CanvasTexture sampled via the texture NODE (raw getTexture() returns black — must use the texture node, per the spike notes). If anything DOES need a node-material swap for parity/perf, the mechanical map is meshBasicMaterial→MeshBasicNodeMaterial, meshStandardMaterial→MeshStandardNodeMaterial, lineBasicMaterial→LineBasicNodeMaterial (imports from three/webgpu) — but only do this if runtime verification shows the built-ins actually break here.
triaged.risks: Built-ins generally work under WebGPURenderer, so the main risk is mis-attribution: spending effort 'porting' MaterializedTab when the real washed-out-tabs cause is the WebGPU lighting/tone-mapping/bloom pipeline (no AgX exposure / missing lights) affecting all meshStandardMaterials — verify that first. Secondary, real risk: troika <Text> (heavily used here — code preview, headers, approval body, buttons) may not survive WebGPURenderer's shader patching → blank/black glyphs; this is the single most likely component-local break and needs a live check under ?gpu=webgpu. Per-frame material mutation (large useFrame at 1164-1585 writing mat.color/emissive/opacity on dozens of meshes) is fine under WebGL but multiply-instantiated tabs could add overhead under WebGPU; not a correctness risk. The GLSL createPointFieldMaterial named in the task brief is NOT used by this file (it's BrainPointField's) — no action here for it.

triaged.mountEvidence: SuperbrainScene.tsx:1640 — `<CosmicBackground tier={tier} arrival={arrivalScalarRef} />` is rendered UNCONDITIONALLY (no gate). Unlike its siblings KnowledgeHorizon (L1637 `BEING_MODE!=='points'`), CognitiveGrasp (L1647 `BEING_MODE!=='points'`), MemoryGalaxy (L1652 `BEING_MODE!=='points'`), CosmicBackground has neither a BEING_MODE nor a GPU_MODE guard. So it is live in the official points path AND under the ?gpu=webgpu spike.
triaged.materialKind: Built-in THREE.PointsMaterial (JSX <pointsMaterial>, CosmicBackground.tsx:145) HEAVILY mutated via onBeforeCompile GLSL string injection (L151-267) — vertex + fragment shader rewrites with custom uniforms (uTime/uAtlas/uArrival), custom attributes (aSpriteIndex/aSize), glyph-atlas texture2D sampling, gl_PointSize override, and AdditiveBlending. So: built-in shell, but its actual look is 100% custom GLSL injection.
triaged.whatItRenders: A drifting void starfield where each "star" is a knowledge GLYPH (∑ ∆ ∞ π Ω code/numeral/alpha symbols) sampled from a procedurally drawn 8x8 CanvasTexture atlas (getGlyphAtlas, L29-62). 900/1800/3000 points by tier (STAR_COUNTS L68). The injected vertex shader flies stars straight at the camera with infinite z-wrap (L174-180), applies an INSTANT gravitational pull/dissolve toward the brain core (0,0,0) within a grasp radius that widens with the shared `arrival` scalar (L182-202), and scales near glyphs up by distance (aSize*22*distanceScale, capped) so close ones read as legible symbols (L210-222). Fragment shader samples the atlas cell per aSpriteIndex, alpha-masks to a soft disc, and fades on dissolve (L236-265). Approval-hold dilates the field clock to ~30% during supervised review (L120-141). Additive blending = the bright washed-out look.
triaged.portStrategy: Port to a SpriteNodeMaterial (three/webgpu) instanced field, mirroring lib/gpuPointFieldTSL.ts (buildGpuPointField) — NOT compute, this is a cheap static field so a render-only TSL graph is enough. Concrete map: (1) Keep the seeded BufferGeometry generation (createSeededRandom 0x564f5941) producing position/color/aSpriteIndex/aSize, but feed them as StorageInstancedBufferAttribute / instanced attributes read in TSL via storage(...).toAttribute() (pattern: gpuPointFieldTSL.ts L60-65, L136-137). Use THREE.Sprite + mat.count=count (L149-150) for instancing rather than THREE.Points (SpriteNodeMaterial is the node-material sprite path per the TSL facts). (2) positionNode: a Fn that reproduces the vertex GLSL — forward-fly `z += time*speed` with `mod` wrap, the smoothstep grasp `mix(25,42,uArrival)` -> pow(pull,1.5) -> mix(pos, vec3(0), snap). Use `time` node + `uniform(uArrival)` (uArrival driven from arrivalScalarRef each frame, like the existing useFrame). Import Fn/uniform/time/mix/clamp/pow/smoothstep/length/float/vec3 from 'three/tsl' (same imports as gpuPointFieldTSL.ts L14-17). (3) scaleNode: replicate gl_PointSize math (aSize attribute * distanceScale, *(1 - vPull*0.8)); SpriteNodeMaterial sizes are world-space so retune constants vs the screen-space `sizeAttenuation={false}` original (expect tuning — note the GPU field already needed uSize/0.06 retuning). (4) colorNode: sample the glyph atlas. CRITICAL TSL fact — raw getTexture() returns black; use the texture NODE (`texture(glyphAtlasTexture, uvNode)`), building the atlas-cell uv from aSpriteIndex (col=mod(idx,8), row=floor(idx/8), uv=(pointCoord+col)/8). Sprites give 0..1 uv() so use uv() in place of gl_PointCoord. Apply the soft-disc alpha mask + dissolve fade, output vec4 with additive blending so AgX Bloom in GpuPostFX picks it up. (5) Keep the CanvasTexture atlas generator and approval-hold/useFrame logic as-is. Cleanest implementation: a parallel CosmicBackgroundGPU.tsx that builds the TSL field (so the WebGL CosmicBackground stays byte-stable), selected at the mount site by GPU_MODE === 'webgpu', exactly like the BrainPointField vs GpuBrainPointField split at SuperbrainScene.tsx:905/919.
triaged.risks: 1) It MOUNTS under WebGPU today (ungated) and silently degrades — the onBeforeCompile GLSL is dropped by WebGPURenderer, leaving plain pointsMaterial dots = the "faint dots" seen in screenshots (no glyphs, no flight, no funnel, no near-magnification). 2) Texture-node gotcha: must use the texture node, not getTexture(), or atlas samples read black (whole field invisible). 3) Sizing model differs: original uses screen-space sizeAttenuation=false + gl_PointSize clamp; SpriteNodeMaterial is world-space scaleNode — sizes/grasp constants will need retuning against the live :5173?gpu=webgpu view (same retuning churn the GPU brain field hit). 4) AdditiveBlending + bloom can white-wash (the GPU brain field already fought this with uGlowMul 0.055 / size 0.6) — watch overall brightness. 5) Per OPERATOR PRIORITY this is cosmos/overlay = second after the materialized TABS and in-brain NODE LATTICE; it does materially fix the "washed-out background" but ship the P0 items first. 6) Quick interim mitigation (not a port): if WebGPU look needs to be acceptable before the port lands, the field could be GPU_MODE-gated off, but that removes the knowledge-glyph identity, so the port is the right fix.

triaged.whatItRenders: A single -16 z-depth backdrop plane (planeGeometry 60x14) positioned at [0,-3.0,-16], rendering a wide, soft horizontal glow band: brightest at the horizon line (vUv.y~0.5, gaussian falloff exp(-pow((vUv.y-0.5)/0.16,2))), feathered at the left/right edges (smoothstep on vUv.x), additive-blended, very faint (default opacity 0.16, color #3a1f7a indigo). A live-tunable atmospheric depth band far behind the being, opacity driven by window.__GAGOS.horizon dial.
triaged.mountEvidence: NOT MOUNTED anywhere. Grep for "HorizonGlow" across frontend/src matches ONLY its own definition file — there is no import or JSX usage in SuperbrainScene.tsx or any other file. The removal is explicitly documented at SuperbrainScene.tsx:1611-1613: "Clean knowledgeable void — no horizon/atmosphere layer (operator: remove the translucent layer from the space). Identity/status live in the 2D GagosChrome layer." It does not mount in points mode, mesh mode, or under the ?gpu=webgpu spike. (Confirms memory note 2223/2236: the plane went edge-on as OrbitControls auto-rotated, then was removed.)
triaged.materialKind: custom GLSL ShaderMaterial (THREE.ShaderMaterial, AdditiveBlending, transparent, depthWrite/depthTest false, toneMapped false; uniforms uColor/uOpacity; inline VERT/FRAG GLSL strings at HorizonGlow.tsx:9-25)
triaged.portStrategy: NO PORT NEEDED — the component is orphaned (zero imports) and was deliberately removed from the scene per operator direction (SuperbrainScene.tsx:1611-1613, "remove the translucent layer from the space"). It never enters the WebGPU render path because it is never mounted, so its GLSL ShaderMaterial cannot cause the washed-out-tabs / invisible-lattice symptom the operator saw. Recommendation: leave as-is or delete the dead file in cleanup; do not spend port effort. (If ever revived, it is a trivial TSL job: MeshBasicNodeMaterial with colorNode = uColor.mul(bandAlpha) and opacityNode = bandAlpha, where bandAlpha is built from uv() with the same exp/smoothstep node graph, blending=AdditiveBlending, transparent/depthWrite false — but this is hypothetical, not part of the current goal.)
triaged.risks: None for the port goal — component is dead code, not on any render path (WebGL or WebGPU). The only standing risk is cosmetic dead-code rot: it still defines an inline window.__GAGOS dial type duplicate (memory 2217/2223) and lingers as an orphaned file. Reviving it under WebGPU without a TSL rewrite would reintroduce a GLSL ShaderMaterial that fails to compile under WebGPURenderer, but that is not in scope.

triaged.mountEvidence: Gated ON in points mode: MaterializationLayer.tsx:430 `{POINTS && orchestration.surfaces.filter(... lifecycle==='retracting').map(... <ReabsorptionParticles/>)}`, where `const POINTS = readBeingMode() === 'points'` (MaterializationLayer.tsx:56). The comment at lines 428-429 confirms it is the points-being's phase-7 reabsorption effect. JSX instance at MaterializationLayer.tsx:434-440. Mounts one instance per retracting non-input tab.
triaged.materialKind: Built-in THREE.PointsMaterial via R3F `<pointsMaterial>` (ReabsorptionParticles.tsx:83-91). NOT a custom shader. Animation is pure CPU: useFrame rewrites BufferGeometry position attribute (lines 55-79) + directly mutates mat.opacity (line 78). No GLSL, no onBeforeCompile, no uniforms.
triaged.whatItRenders: ~72 (COUNT=72) additive-blended point sprites, default color #5ef0b0 (green), size 0.05 with sizeAttenuation, depthWrite=false. They spawn scattered around a retracting tab's vertebra anchor (origin) with per-mote birth stagger, puff outward, then ease-rise and converge toward the spine centerline up into the brain at target [0,0.1,0], brightening on departure and fading to 0 opacity on arrival over 1300ms. Visualizes "work completes -> tab dissolves into motes -> energy streams up the spine into the brain."
triaged.portStrategy: No TSL port required. PointsMaterial is a NON-shader built-in that WebGPURenderer supports through its WebGL-compat node path (per the established TSL facts: LineBasicMaterial/MeshBasicMaterial/PointsMaterial generally work; only custom GLSL ShaderMaterial/RawShaderMaterial/onBeforeCompile break). All per-frame work here is renderer-agnostic three.js core API: `geometry.attributes.position.needsUpdate=true` (CPU buffer update) and `mat.opacity=` mutation — both valid under WebGPURenderer with no changes. Recommendation: leave AS-IS; verify visually under ?gpu=webgpu by triggering a tab retract. ONLY if WebGPU PointsMaterial round-sprite/sizeAttenuation rendering proves visually off (a fidelity nuance, not a crash) would a swap to SpriteNodeMaterial be warranted — and in that case reuse the SpriteNodeMaterial + storage-buffer pattern from lib/gpuPointFieldTSL.ts and GpuBrainPointField.tsx. But for only 72 CPU-animated motes, a TSL/compute rewrite would be over-engineering; keep the simple CPU points path.
triaged.risks: Low. (1) Built-in PointsMaterial is expected to work under WebGPURenderer but should be confirmed live (trigger a tab reabsorption under ?gpu=webgpu) since round-point + sizeAttenuation can render subtly differently than WebGL — fidelity nuance, not breakage. (2) Per-frame `position.needsUpdate` triggers a GPU buffer re-upload each frame; fine at 72 motes x small tab count. (3) Only mounts during the brief 1300ms retracting window per tab, so it is rarely on screen — easy to miss when QA-ing the WebGPU path. Not related to the operator's reported breakages (washed-out tabs / invisible node lattice use GLSL ShaderMaterial elsewhere).

triaged.whatItRenders: The brain GLB cortex skin: a near-black glass MeshPhysicalMaterial (color 0x010308, clearcoat 1.0) wearing baked region vertex-colours, an animated 3D-Voronoi "neural web" raised into glowing synapse lines, procedural bump (dFdx/dFdy normal perturbation), per-region fresnel rim, and emission-driven pulsing on uTime/uHold/uArrival/uIgnite. In NODE_BRAIN mode the material is set near-transparent (opacity 0.16, depthWrite false) and microDetail returns 0.0 — i.e. it is meant to become a quiet glass cranium so the NodeLattice glows through. The neuralSkin shell (a sibling custom ShaderMaterial, brainMaterial sibling at SuperbrainScene.tsx:757-764) rides the same GLB.
triaged.mountEvidence: Cortex GLB mounts ONLY under BEING_MODE==='mesh': SuperbrainScene.tsx:897-903 ({BEING_MODE === 'mesh' && (surface==='organ' ? <OrganSurface/> : <primitive object={brainAsset.object}/>)}) and the neuralSkin shell at :904 ({BEING_MODE === 'mesh' && <primitive object={neuralSkin.object} scale={1.004}/>}). In points mode (the official frontend) those primitives are gated OFF; the brain+fused-spine are rendered instead by the already-ported GpuBrainPointField at :905-918 (webgpu) / BrainPointField at :919-933 (webgl). NOTE: makeBrainMaterial is still CONSTRUCTED in points mode (brainAsset useMemo at :730-750, with nodeBrain=NODE_BRAIN=true at :725/:741) but the mesh that would wear it is never added to the scene graph — so the material's nodeBrain glass-cranium + microDetail-strip branches (brainMaterial.ts:156-160, :279) are dead code in the points path. The brainAsset.object IS still passed as a geometry SOURCE to GpuBrainPointField (source={brainAsset.object} at :911), but only its geometry/vertices are sampled to seed particles — its material is never used by the GPU point path.
triaged.materialKind: Built-in THREE.MeshPhysicalMaterial mutated via onBeforeCompile GLSL injection (custom #include splicing: common/begin_vertex/normal_fragment_begin/color_fragment/emissivemap_fragment) — i.e. GLSL onBeforeCompile, NOT a node material. Sibling neuralSkin is a raw custom THREE.ShaderMaterial (CASING_VERTEX/FRAGMENT_SHADER).
triaged.portStrategy: SKIP — do not port. The material would break under WebGPU (onBeforeCompile GLSL injection is not honored by WebGPURenderer; uses hash33/voronoi, dFdx/dFdy, custom varyings, #include splicing — all GLSL-only), but this is irrelevant because the mesh that wears it is gated to BEING_MODE==='mesh' and NEVER mounts in the official points/WebGPU path. Porting it fixes no operator-visible breakage. The washed-out/invisible things the operator saw are (a) the materialized TABS and (b) the in-brain NODE LATTICE — the latter is the separate NodeLattice component (SuperbrainScene.tsx:963-965), which DOES mount unconditionally in points (NODE_BRAIN=true) and uses three custom GLSL ShaderMaterials (nodeMat/edgeMat/backboneMat) — that is the true P0, triaged separately. CONTINGENCY ONLY (not in scope now): if the operator later wants a literal glass-cranium shell framing the point-being under WebGPU, build it as a NEW small component — a thin MeshBasicNodeMaterial with a fresnel rim (oneMinus(dot(normalView, positionViewDirection)).pow(2.5) via three/tsl), opacity ~0.16, no Voronoi/bump/emission — mounted conditionally around the point-field. Do not attempt to translate the full Voronoi/bump emission ladder; in NODE_BRAIN that ladder is already stripped to nothing (microDetail returns 0.0) so there is nothing meaningful to reproduce.
triaged.risks: Low risk in skipping: confirmed by both live JSX gating (BEING_MODE==='mesh' at lines 897/904) and prior deep-dive observation 3175. Main risk is a wrong-target trap: an agent could waste high effort porting this elaborate Voronoi/bump/fresnel GLSL when it never renders in points. Caveat to flag to the orchestrator: makeBrainMaterial is also used by NervousSystem.tsx:365 (bodyMode 'nerve' for the cord/vertebrae/roots) — that is a DIFFERENT mount; triage NervousSystem separately to learn whether the mesh nerve geometry mounts in points (likely also mesh-gated since the spine is the GpuBrainPointField in points, but verify). Also the third makeBrainMaterial call at SuperbrainScene.tsx:1004 (PointerBrainClone, nodeBrain:false) and primitive at :1110 should be checked for its own gating but is a pointer-hover mesh clone, almost certainly mesh-only too.

triaged.whatItRenders: A mesh-era accretion disk: an 820-point swirling infall field (THREE.Points) that orbits/spirals into the brain core to read as "matter being absorbed" — the disc is the being's "stomach" that brightens (uPulse 1.0→1.5) on knowledge-acquired cognition events. Renders behind the MESH brain at group position [0, 0.12, -1.18] with a fixed tilt. It is an OVERLAY for the mesh being only; the comment at SuperbrainScene.tsx:1672 states "in points mode the cloud is the being," i.e. the points-being itself replaces this disk.
triaged.mountEvidence: SuperbrainScene.tsx:1673-1675 — gated `{BEING_MODE !== 'points' && (<AccretionCore .../>)}`. BEING_MODE is a module-level const `readBeingMode()` at SuperbrainScene.tsx:73; the official frontend runs points mode, so the gate is false and the component is never mounted. The WebGPU spike itself only runs under points mode (SuperbrainScene.tsx:905 `BEING_MODE === 'points' && GPU_MODE === 'webgpu'`), so under ?gpu=webgpu AccretionCore is definitively absent.
triaged.materialKind: custom GLSL ShaderMaterial (raw vertexShader DISK_VERTEX_SHADER + fragmentShader DISK_FRAGMENT_SHADER at AccretionCore.tsx:35-121, instantiated via <shaderMaterial> at lines 254-263 with uniforms uFlowTime/uActivity/uBurst/uPulse/uArrival/uPostureColor/uPostureTint, AdditiveBlending, gl_PointSize/gl_PointCoord point sprites)
triaged.portStrategy: NO PORT NEEDED for the WebGPU goal. The component is hard-gated OFF in points mode (BEING_MODE !== 'points', SuperbrainScene.tsx:1673) and the WebGPU spike only exists in points mode — so AccretionCore never mounts in the WebGPU path and cannot contribute to the broken washed-out tabs / invisible lattice the operator saw. Its GLSL ShaderMaterial WOULD break under WebGPU if it ever mounted (custom vertex/fragment GLSL, gl_PointSize, gl_PointCoord, gl_FragColor, custom uniforms = no WebGL-compat path), but that is irrelevant here. If a future redesign ever wants this disk in the points-being: it is a near-exact match for the existing TSL reference — port to SpriteNodeMaterial (lib/gpuPointFieldTSL.ts pattern) with positionNode computing the swirl/infall (Fn graph over per-particle storage/attribute buffers aAngle/aSpeed/aPhase/aSize/aTint/aJitter), scaleNode for gl_PointSize, colorNode for the accretionColor ramp + ember/flash/pulse, opacityNode for vAlpha, uniform() nodes for uFlowTime/uActivity/uBurst/uPulse/uArrival; additive blending + transparent. But this is speculative future work, not part of the current port goal.
triaged.risks: Only risk is a false skip: if the operator ever flips the official frontend back to mesh mode (BEING_MODE==='mesh') AND adds WebGPU support for mesh mode, this would surface as a broken washed-out disk. Currently impossible — mesh+WebGPU is not a wired combination (WebGPU spike is points-only, SuperbrainScene.tsx:905). No action required for the stated port goal.

triaged.whatItRenders: ~320 (tier-scaled 80/180/320) surface-sampled "synaptic firefly" point motes (THREE.Points) lifted ~0.004 off the cortex surface of the brain mesh. Each mote blinks like a synapse (pow-8 narrowed sine flash) and fires preferentially where a thought-wave passes, sharing the cortex's uWaveOrigins/uWaveTimes uniforms. Soft additive glow with hot core, occluded by the cortex (depthTest on). A mesh-era overlay decoration for the organ/mesh brain.
triaged.mountEvidence: Gated OFF in points mode: SuperbrainScene.tsx:950 wraps the only mount in `{BEING_MODE !== 'points' && (<CorticalSignals .../>)}` (element at 951-957). BEING_MODE = readBeingMode() (SuperbrainScene.tsx:73); official GAGOS runs points. No alternate `BEING_MODE === 'points'` re-mount exists for this component anywhere in the file (verified via BEING_MODE grep — only the single negated block references it).
triaged.materialKind: custom GLSL ShaderMaterial (<shaderMaterial> at CorticalSignals.tsx:262-272, FIREFLY_VERTEX_SHADER/FIREFLY_FRAGMENT_SHADER, raw GLSL using gl_PointSize/gl_Position/gl_PointCoord/gl_FragColor + shared THOUGHT_WAVE_GLSL thoughtWave())
triaged.portStrategy: No port required for the WebGPU goal: the component is gated off in points mode (SuperbrainScene.tsx:950, BEING_MODE !== 'points') and never mounts in the official GAGOS path, so its custom GLSL ShaderMaterial never executes under WebGPURenderer. IF it were ever re-enabled in points mode, it would break under WebGPU (raw GLSL vertex/fragment shaders + gl_* builtins are unsupported) and would need a TSL port: convert to SpriteNodeMaterial (per GpuBrainPointField.tsx / lib/gpuPointFieldTSL.ts pattern) with positionLocal from a storage/instanced attribute buffer for the sampled cortex positions; port THOUGHT_WAVE_GLSL thoughtWave() to a Fn() node consuming uniform arrays uWaveOrigins/uWaveTimes; map aColor/aPhase/aSpeed/aSize to instanced attributes; replicate the pow-8 flash + proximity bias in the color/opacity node; reuse AdditiveBlending + depthTest via the node material flags. Not on the critical path — skip.
triaged.risks: Low/none for the port goal — it does not render in points mode so it cannot be the cause of the washed-out tabs or invisible node lattice the operator saw. Only risk is a future regression: if someone re-mounts cortical fireflies in points mode (e.g. as a points-being cortex decoration) without porting to TSL, they will silently vanish/error under ?gpu=webgpu. The shared THOUGHT_WAVE_GLSL export is also imported by SuperbrainScene's cortex shader (line 10) — that cortex path is likewise mesh-gated, so no WebGPU exposure today.

triaged.mountEvidence: Gated OFF in points mode at SuperbrainScene.tsx:940 — `{tier !== 'low' && BEING_MODE !== 'points' && (<NeuralAura .../>)}`. The `BEING_MODE !== 'points'` clause excludes it from the official points path AND from the WebGPU spike (which is itself a points path: line 905 `BEING_MODE === 'points' && GPU_MODE === 'webgpu'`). Comment at SuperbrainScene.tsx:938-939 confirms: "Mesh-era brain overlays — in points mode the point cloud IS the being, so these are gated off". Imported at SuperbrainScene.tsx:16.
triaged.materialKind: Three custom GLSL THREE.ShaderMaterial instances: membrane shell (NeuralAura.tsx:278-288, BackSide additive), core/nucleus shell (289-300, FrontSide additive, renderOrder -1), and a synaptic-sparks <shaderMaterial> on a <points> (407-416). All raw hand-written GLSL vertex+fragment strings (SHELL_VERTEX_SHADER, MEMBRANE_FRAGMENT_SHADER, CORE_FRAGMENT_SHADER at 67-136; SPARK_VERTEX/FRAGMENT_SHADER at 179-231). No built-in materials.
triaged.whatItRenders: A two-shell fake-volume "atmosphere" around the mesh-era brain plus orbiting synaptic sparks. Shell 1 (membrane, scale 1.025, BackSide): a thin region-tinted halo hugging the cortex silhouette via inverse-fresnel `pow(max(0.6 - dot(N,V),0),4)` capped at 0.25 alpha. Shell 2 (nucleus, scale 0.85, FrontSide, drawn before cortex): a rose SSS interior glow `pow(clamp(dot(N,V),0,1),2)` ghosting through the cortex. Both clone the processed brain GLB geometry (shared color attribute + smooth normals) and breathe/burst via shared CognitionUniforms (uBreath/uBurst/uArrival). Plus 50 point sparks orbiting on great circles (SPARK_ORBIT_RADIUS 0.48), mode-reactive color (observe cyan / synthesize white / orchestrate violet, amber on approval hold), flickering per-spark.
triaged.portStrategy: NO PORT NEEDED for the operator's goal — NeuralAura is gated off in points mode (BEING_MODE !== 'points' at SuperbrainScene.tsx:940) so it never mounts in the official frontend or the ?gpu=webgpu spike. It is a pure mesh-era (BEING_MODE === 'mesh') overlay. Porting it would be dead work that does not affect the broken work-tabs or in-brain node lattice. IF the mesh era were ever revived under WebGPU, the three custom GLSL ShaderMaterials would each need conversion: shells → NodeMaterial (three/webgpu) with view-space normal + Fn fresnel node graphs feeding colorNode/opacityNode driven by uniform(uBreath/uBurst/uArrival) leaves, vertexColors via attribute('color'); sparks → SpriteNodeMaterial with a Fn positionNode building the great-circle orbit from per-instance axis/phase/speed attributes (pattern in lib/gpuPointFieldTSL.ts + GpuBrainPointField.tsx). But this is out of scope.
triaged.risks: Low/none for the port goal: component is dead in points mode, so leaving it un-ported is correct and zero-risk. The only risk is wasting effort porting a gated-off mesh-era overlay. Confirm BEING_MODE stays 'points' (it is the official default per memory); if the mesh era is ever resurrected under WebGPU this would resurface as a 3-shader port (medium effort: two NodeMaterial fresnel shells + one SpriteNodeMaterial orbit), but that is not the current goal.

triaged.whatItRenders: A particle galaxy of orbiting "skill-stars" — one persistent point per REAL pheromone trail (getKnownTrails), deterministically placed from skill_id. A single THREE.Points (line 189) with up to MAX_STARS=128 instances. Per-star attributes (aRadius/aAngle/aSpeed/aHeight/aWobble/aSize/aStrength/aQuarantine/aFlash) encode orbit + trail data; the GLSL vertex shader animates orbits (cos/sin of aAngle + uTime*aSpeed) and computes gl_PointSize with a flash decay exp(-max(uTime-aFlash,0)*1.6); the fragment shader draws a soft additive nucleus (smoothstep on gl_PointCoord), twinkle, sRGB->linear cyan (0.3424,0.7484,1.0) tinting toward pulsing red on quarantine, alpha scaled by strength + flash. Dormant (returns null) until real memory exists (starCount===0).
triaged.mountEvidence: SuperbrainScene.tsx:1652 `{SHOW_MEMORY_GALAXY && BEING_MODE !== 'points' && <MemoryGalaxy />}`. SHOW_MEMORY_GALAXY=true (line 70) but BEING_MODE=readBeingMode() (line 73) is 'points' in the official frontend, so the `BEING_MODE !== 'points'` clause is false and the component never mounts. (Imported at line 20; this is the only JSX usage.)
triaged.materialKind: custom GLSL THREE.ShaderMaterial (vertexShader=GALAXY_VERTEX, fragmentShader=GALAXY_FRAGMENT, AdditiveBlending, transparent, depthWrite:false) — constructed at MemoryGalaxy.tsx:114-121 with uniforms {uTime} and 9 custom per-vertex float attributes.
triaged.portStrategy: No port needed for the GAGOS WebGPU goal: the component is hard-gated `BEING_MODE !== 'points'` (SuperbrainScene.tsx:1652) and never mounts in the official points-mode frontend, so it cannot render the washed-out/invisible artifacts the operator saw. IF it were ever ungated for points mode, it is a mesh-era GLSL ShaderMaterial that would break under WebGPURenderer and would need a TSL rewrite: replace ShaderMaterial with a SpriteNodeMaterial (transparent, blending=AdditiveBlending, depthWrite=false) following the GpuBrainPointField/gpuPointFieldTSL.ts pattern; carry the 9 attributes as instanced storage/attribute nodes (or compute them with a tiny TSL Fn from a per-star seed like aAngle/skill_id hash); a uniform('uTime') drives the orbit (positionLocal via cos/sin of aAngle + uTime*aSpeed) and the flash decay; map the fragment to colorNode/opacityNode using uv()/sprite coord for the smoothstep nucleus + twinkle + quarantine red mix; keep the existing cognitionBus subscribe + getKnownTrails sync logic untouched (it only writes buffer attributes). Reuse curlNoiseTSL.ts only if drift is desired; not required here. This is a low-priority hypothetical — leave as-is.
triaged.risks: None for the current goal — gated off in points mode, so it is dead code on the official WebGPU path. Caveat: SHOW_MEMORY_GALAXY=true means the ONLY thing keeping it out of points mode is the `BEING_MODE !== 'points'` clause; if a future change removes that clause to revive the galaxy in points mode, the GLSL ShaderMaterial will silently break (flat/missing stars) under ?gpu=webgpu and would then need the TSL port above. Also a known pre-existing functional bug (unrelated to WebGPU): BUG-A — the `trail #(\d+)` flash regex at line 172 reportedly does not flash the mastery star (per memory observations 837/1593).

triaged.whatItRenders: A photographic deep-space sky dome: a BackSide sphereGeometry (radius 90) at renderOrder -2 (background). The fragment shader draws an IQ domain-warped fbm nebula (q->r->f warp colored by intermediate warp vectors), a Hubble-SHO linear-space palette ramp, ridged striation + dark dust lanes (occlusion), one diagonal galactic band, a multi-layer photographic star field (cell-hash placement, pow(hash,9) brightness law, blackbody tints, twinkle, diffraction spikes), three-layer pointer parallax, and IGN dither. Pure cosmos backdrop behind the brain.
triaged.mountEvidence: Gated OFF in points mode. SuperbrainScene.tsx:1637 — `{sky === 'layered' && tier !== 'low' && BEING_MODE !== 'points' && <KnowledgeHorizon activity={activeBoost} />}`. The `BEING_MODE !== 'points'` clause excludes it from the official points path. Reinforced by the inline comment at SuperbrainScene.tsx:1611-1613: in points mode the operator deliberately renders a "Clean knowledgeable void — no horizon/atmosphere layer (operator: remove the translucent layer from the space)". Single import at line 19, single JSX usage at line 1638.
triaged.materialKind: custom GLSL ShaderMaterial (R3F <shaderMaterial> with raw vertexShader/fragmentShader strings) at KnowledgeHorizon.tsx:289-297
triaged.portStrategy: NO PORT NEEDED for the GAGOS WebGPU goal. The component never mounts in points mode (the official frontend), so under ?gpu=webgpu it is not in the scene graph and cannot cause the washed-out/invisible regressions the operator saw. Material-wise it WOULD break under WebGPU if forced (custom GLSL string shaders using fwidth/gl_FragColor/gl_FragCoord/highp precision — none of which the WebGPU node pipeline accepts), but that is moot here. If at some future point the operator wants the photographic dome back under WebGPU in points mode, the port would be a heavy lift: rewrite the entire ~200-line fragment shader as a TSL Fn graph on a NodeMaterial/MeshBasicNodeMaterial (BackSide sphere), replace gl_FragColor with colorNode, gl_FragCoord with screenCoordinate, fwidth with dFdx/dFdy length, the hash/valueNoise/fbm3 helpers with TSL Fn equivalents (or reuse curlNoiseTSL.ts patterns from the spike), and pass uTime/uActivity/uPointer as uniform() nodes — but this is NOT in scope for the stated goal. Mark skip.
triaged.risks: None for the port goal — gated off in points mode, so no WebGPU breakage path. Memory confirms the operator already removed the horizon/atmosphere layer from the void in points mode by design, so reintroducing it (e.g. via a TSL port) would CONTRADICT a deliberate aesthetic decision — do not port it back without his explicit sign-off. Only verification caveat: the gate is a triple AND (sky==='layered' && tier!=='low' && BEING_MODE!=='points'); BEING_MODE!=='points' alone is sufficient to exclude it from the official path, so the conclusion holds regardless of sky/tier.

triaged.whatItRenders: A mesh-era "knowledge recall" overlay: 4 distant glint TARGETS (wire-cage octahedra + pale gold core crystals) scattered at scene depth, with animated retrieval PACKETS that travel out along routed Bezier-ish paths to a target and return to the brain, firing a cortical "RECALL burst" on the cognitionBus when a packet is absorbed. Each glint is data-bound to a REAL AI-OS pheromone trail (strength brightens core, walk-count grows cage, freshness quickens spin, quarantine stains cage red). Returns null when no real trails are known (honest dormancy).
triaged.mountEvidence: SuperbrainScene.tsx:1647 — `{tier !== 'low' && BEING_MODE !== 'points' && <CognitiveGrasp activity={activeBoost} />}`. BEING_MODE is the module-level const `BEING_MODE = readBeingMode()` (SuperbrainScene.tsx:73). The official GAGOS frontend runs readBeingMode()==='points', so the `BEING_MODE !== 'points'` guard evaluates false and CognitiveGrasp is never instantiated in the points path. (Additionally gated by tier !== 'low'.)
triaged.materialKind: Mixed. ONE custom GLSL ShaderMaterial (the route point-filament: routeVertexShader/routeFragmentShader, CognitiveGrasp.tsx:255-301, mounted via <shaderMaterial> at lines 494-503 with AdditiveBlending + custom aProgress attribute). Everything else is built-ins: <lineBasicMaterial> for the wire cages, and many <meshBasicMaterial> for the glint cores/halos, launch flare ring+core, outbound/return packets, and receive rings.
triaged.portStrategy: NO PORT NEEDED for the GAGOS WebGPU goal — the component is gated OFF in points mode (BEING_MODE !== 'points' at SuperbrainScene.tsx:1647), so it never mounts on the official frontend and never appears under ?gpu=webgpu. It is purely mesh-era. (If it were ever re-enabled in points mode, only the single route-filament GLSL ShaderMaterial at CognitiveGrasp.tsx:255-301/494-503 would BREAK under WebGPU and need a TSL port: a PointsNodeMaterial/SpriteNodeMaterial driven by an aProgress attribute() node, with sizeNode mapping the gl_PointSize formula and colorNode/opacityNode mapping the packetTrain/travelingFocus/reveal math via Fn + uniform()s for uActivity/uOpacity/uReveal/uSeed/uTime, plus a circular sprite mask — mirror lib/gpuPointFieldTSL.ts SpriteNodeMaterial. The lineBasicMaterial cages + meshBasicMaterial glints/packets/rings would render fine on WebGPURenderer's built-in compat path with no changes.)
triaged.risks: Minimal. The mount guard is an unambiguous compile-time-ish const comparison; very low chance of mounting in the official path. Only residual concern: if the operator later flips this overlay ON in points mode, the route-filament GLSL would silently render as nothing/black under WebGPU (custom ShaderMaterial does not run on WebGPURenderer) — but the rest (cages/glints/packets via Line/MeshBasicMaterial) would still appear. Not a current-goal blocker.

roadmap.summary: The real WebGPU port surface is TINY: of the 5 triaged components, only TWO actually need TSL ports, and only ONE of those is in the operator's P0 priority. NodeLattice (the in-brain node network) is the single P0 break: 3 custom GLSL ShaderMaterials that WebGPURenderer silently drops, leaving the lattice invisible — verified at NodeLattice.tsx:706 (nodeMat), :779 (edgeMat), :831 (backboneMat), all AdditiveBlending/depthTest:false. CosmicBackground is the P1 break: a built-in pointsMaterial whose entire look is onBeforeCompile GLSL injection (CosmicBackground.tsx:151), which WebGPURenderer ignores → glyph starfield degrades to faint plain dots; mounted UNGATED at SuperbrainScene.tsx:1640. The operator's OTHER reported symptom — washed-out flat TABS — is almost certainly MIS-ATTRIBUTED: MaterializedTab contains ZERO custom shaders (grep for ShaderMaterial/onBeforeCompile = 0 matches), only three.js built-ins (meshStandardMaterial/meshBasicMaterial/lineBasicMaterial/troika Text), which DO render under WebGPU's compat path. The washed-out look is the global lighting/AgX-tone-mapping/bloom pipeline (GpuPostFX), NOT a per-tab material — so Phase 1 must include a runtime VERIFY of the tabs (lights + AgX + troika glyph survival under ?gpu=webgpu) rather than a blind port. HorizonGlow is dead code (never imported). ReabsorptionParticles already works (plain CPU-animated pointsMaterial). Net: build a shared additive sprite/wire TSL toolkit ONCE, port NodeLattice's 3 materials (P0) + CosmicBackground's 1 (P1) against it, dual-path so the WebGL default never regresses.
activePortTargets: NodeLattice (3 GLSL ShaderMaterials → TSL node materials) — the in-brain node network, OPERATOR P0, invisible under WebGPU
CosmicBackground (pointsMaterial + onBeforeCompile GLSL → SpriteNodeMaterial TSL field) — knowledge-glyph starfield, P1, degrades to faint dots under WebGPU
skipList: MaterializedTab — SKIP the GLSL→TSL port (zero custom shaders; all three.js built-ins). Mounts ungated in points (MaterializationLayer.tsx:413-427) and was seen washed-out, but cause is the global WebGPU lighting/AgX/bloom path (GpuPostFX), not these materials. Action = RUNTIME VERIFY (lights+AgX reach this layer; troika <Text> glyphs survive WebGPURenderer patching), NOT a port.
ReabsorptionParticles — already WORKS under WebGPU (plain built-in <pointsMaterial>, CPU per-frame mutation, no shader injection). Visual confirm only.
HorizonGlow — DEAD CODE, never imported/mounted (removed per SuperbrainScene.tsx:1611-1613). Never on any render path → no port. Optional cleanup-delete.
KnowledgeHorizon / CognitiveGrasp / MemoryGalaxy — GATED OFF in points (SuperbrainScene.tsx:1637/1647/1652, BEING_MODE !== 'points'); never render in official path → no port.
NeuralAura / CorticalSignals — mesh-era overlays gated BEING_MODE !== 'points' (SuperbrainScene.tsx:940/950); never mount in points → out of scope.
roadmap.sharedInfra: Build a small TSL toolkit ONCE, reusing the proven patterns in lib/gpuPointFieldTSL.ts + GpuBrainPointField.tsx + GpuPostFX.tsx, so the two ports do not duplicate: (1) An ADDITIVE-SPRITE node-material factory (SpriteNodeMaterial, AdditiveBlending, depthWrite/depthTest:false, toneMapped:false, emit >1 so GpuPostFX bloom picks it up) — consumed by BOTH the NodeLattice glow-spheres AND the CosmicBackground glyph field. (2) A FRESNEL Fn (pow(1 - dot(normalGeometry, normalView clamp), k)) for the lattice node core+rim. (3) THE WIRE/PACKET Fn — the single highest-value shared piece: NodeLattice's edgeMat (NodeLattice.tsx:779) and backboneMat (:831) share the IDENTICAL WIRE_FRAGMENT fiber-optic packet+arrival-wipe math, and it is noted as a copy of NervousSystem's shader. Translate the packet/carrier/wipe (carrier=color*gain; flow=fract(uv.x*4 - time*speed*motion + phase); packet=smoothstep band; arrival wipe=step(uv.x,front)) to ONE TSL Fn, parameterized by attribute(aWireColor/aPhase/aSpeed) + uniforms — reused by both lattice tube materials and revivable for NervousSystem. (4) A DISCARD→MASK helper: WGSL has no `discard`; both the wire endpoint cut and the glyph soft-disc must become a multiplicative alpha mask + transparent/opacity so masked fragments don't stack additively. (5) An INSTANCED-ATTRIBUTE access helper (storage(...).element(instanceIndex)/instancedBufferAttribute) for per-instance color + the dynamic aActivation/aIgniteDelay buffers, mirroring gpuPointFieldTSL.ts:53-65. (6) A GLYPH-ATLAS texture-node sampler (texture(atlas, cellUv) — NOT getTexture(), which returns black) shared if any other glyph field is added. (7) A uniform()-bridge that feeds the existing CognitionUniforms refs (uTime/uBurst/uHold/uArrival/uReducedMotion/uSignalGain/uCarrierGain/uNodeGain) into TSL nodes. ALL non-material logic (buildLatticeData topology, real-data fetch, subscribeCognition, activation-decay useFrame, the CanvasTexture atlas generator, approval-hold clock) is renderer-agnostic and stays unchanged in both components.

phases.phase: Phase 0 — Shared TSL infra + tab diagnosis (foundation, no user-visible port yet)
phases.rationale: Two of the three NodeLattice shaders share the WIRE packet math and the lattice spheres + cosmos glyphs share the additive-sprite material — building these helpers first means the P0/P1 ports are assembly, not duplicate authoring. CRITICALLY, this phase also resolves the operator's washed-out-TABS report WITHOUT a port: open ?gpu=webgpu and confirm whether the flat look is (a) missing scene lights/AgX exposure on the meshStandard tabs, or (b) troika <Text> glyphs going blank under WebGPURenderer shader-patching. That diagnosis decides whether tabs need a global-pipeline fix (likely) or a localized text fallback (CanvasTexture via texture node) — cheap, and it de-risks Phase 1 by not chasing a phantom port. Reuse refs: lib/gpuPointFieldTSL.ts, GpuBrainPointField.tsx, GpuPostFX.tsx.

phases.phase: Phase 1 — IN-BRAIN NODE LATTICE (OPERATOR P0) + tab pipeline fix
components: NodeLattice (edgeMat → MeshBasicNodeMaterial via shared WIRE Fn)
NodeLattice (backboneMat → MeshBasicNodeMaterial via shared WIRE Fn)
MaterializedTab pipeline fix (lights/AgX or troika fallback, per Phase 0 finding)
phases.rationale: This IS the operator's top-priority break ('in-brain node lattice invisible under WebGPU', confirmed root cause obs 3172). Port all 3 ShaderMaterials onto the Phase-0 helpers: nodes = instanced glow spheres with fresnel core+rim + aActivation surge + aIgniteDelay arrival ignite, emitting >1 for bloom; edges+backbone = the shared WIRE packet Fn with per-tube aWireColor/aPhase/aSpeed attributes. Dual-path/gate by GPU_MODE so the working WebGL lattice is byte-stable (do NOT regress default). Land the tab fix here too since it's the other thing he saw broken and Phase 0 already located it. After this phase both operator-reported symptoms are resolved. Watch: additive+depthTest:false render-order vs the TSL bloom knee (NODE_GAIN/SIGNAL_GAIN luma>1); sacred palette — lift luminance only, never shift baked region hues.

Second per operator priority (cosmos/overlay after tabs+lattice). It mounts UNGATED today (SuperbrainScene.tsx:1640) and silently degrades — WebGPURenderer drops the onBeforeCompile GLSL (CosmicBackground.tsx:151), leaving plain faint dots: no glyphs, no forward-fly, no gravitational funnel, no near-magnification = the 'washed-out background' in the screenshots. Build a parallel CosmicBackgroundGPU.tsx (keeps the WebGL file byte-stable) selected at the mount site by GPU_MODE==='webgpu', mirroring the BrainPointField vs GpuBrainPointField split (SuperbrainScene.tsx:905/919). Reuse the Phase-0 additive-sprite factory + glyph-atlas texture-node sampler; keep the seeded geometry, CanvasTexture atlas generator, and approval-hold clock as-is. positionNode reproduces the forward-fly + smoothstep grasp pull toward (0,0,0); scaleNode replicates the gl_PointSize math (expect retuning: SpriteNodeMaterial is world-space vs the original screen-space sizeAttenuation=false). CRITICAL: sample via texture() node, not getTexture() (returns black).

components: Cross-component additive/bloom brightness reconcile (lattice + cosmos + GpuBrainPointField under one AgX/bloom pass)
Reduced-motion parity check (uReducedMotion path in TSL)
Final ?gpu=webgpu vs default WebGL parity review
phases.rationale: Three additive systems now feed the same GpuPostFX bloom (GPU brain field already fought white-wash with uGlowMul 0.055/size 0.6) — reconcile gains so nothing white-washes and the lattice still POPS past the bloom knee. Confirm reduced-motion freezes packets/funnel in the TSL paths too. Verify the WebGL default is unchanged (sacred palette intact, 209 tests green) and the full ?gpu=webgpu scene matches the operator's :5173 look. Optionally delete the orphaned HorizonGlow.tsx during cleanup.

roadmap.risks: 1) MIS-ATTRIBUTION (biggest risk): the washed-out TABS are likely the global WebGPU lighting/AgX/bloom pipeline, NOT a MaterializedTab material — porting the tab would burn effort and not fix it. Phase 0 verify-first mitigates this. 2) troika <Text> (heavily used in tabs: code preview, headers, approval body, buttons) may not survive WebGPURenderer's shader-chunk patching → blank/black glyphs; this is the single most likely tab-local break and needs a live ?gpu=webgpu check; fallback = CanvasTexture sampled via texture node. 3) WGSL has NO `discard` — both the wire endpoint cut (vUv.x<0.005||>0.995) and the glyph soft-disc must become multiplicative masks; getting masked black fragments to NOT stack additively needs care (build the mask helper once in Phase 0). 4) Additive + depthTest:false render-order vs the TSL bloom must be re-verified so lattice nodes/wires still pop past the knee while three systems share one bloom pass (white-wash risk). 5) Instancing differs under node materials (no #ifdef USE_INSTANCING_COLOR injection): per-instance color + the dynamic aActivation/aIgniteDelay buffers must move to storage()/instancedBufferAttribute via instanceIndex, and instance normals need the node-material instancing path. 6) SpriteNodeMaterial is world-space; the cosmos field's screen-space sizeAttenuation=false sizing/grasp constants WILL need retuning (same churn the GPU brain field hit). 7) Texture-node gotcha: getTexture() returns black — must use the texture() node for the glyph atlas or the whole field is invisible. 8) SACRED PALETTE: region/glyph hues are baked — ports must lift luminance only, never shift hue; keep the WebGL default dual-pathed/byte-stable so check_css_canon and the 209 tests stay green.
